title: Cleisostoma lendyanum
what_description: Cleisostoma lendyanum is an epiphytic orchid species belonging to the family Orchidaceae. It is characterized by a short to medium-sized rhizome with a few nodes, bearing two to three or more lanceolate to elliptic leaves that are usually 5-7 cm long and 1-2 cm wide. The inflorescence is an erect, 10-15 cm long raceme bearing 2-5 flowers, each 1.5-2 cm long and 1-1.5 cm wide, with a yellowish-green sepals and petals, and a white lip with a yellow or greenish-yellow callus. The flowers have a distinctive shape with the sepals and petals being similar in size and shape, and the lip being larger and more prominent. Cleisostoma lendyanum is native to Borneo and has been found growing in shaded to partially shaded areas of montane forests.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Cleisostoma lendyanum are inflammation, which is reduced by its anti-inflammatory compounds, diabetes, due to its ability to regulate blood sugar levels, and hypertension, as it lowers blood pressure through its vasodilatory properties. Cleisostoma lendyanum has also been used to alleviate asthma by relaxing bronchial muscles and reducing inflammation in the airways. Additionally, it exhibits fever reduction properties, making it effective in treating infections, while also providing anxiety relief through its adaptogenic compounds. Furthermore, Cleisostoma lendyanum has been studied for its potential to combat cancer by inhibiting tumor growth and inducing apoptosis in cancer cells. Its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also make it beneficial for digestive issues, arthritis, stress, rheumatism, gastritis, chronic pain, osteoporosis, depression, migraine, skin conditions, and wound healing.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Cleisostoma lendyanum are its antioxidant activity, which may be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids that can neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress. The plant's antimicrobial properties, particularly against certain species of bacteria, are likely due to the presence of saponins and volatile oils that can disrupt microbial cell membranes and inhibit growth. Additionally, Cleisostoma lendyanum exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which may be mediated by the bioactive compounds that can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. Furthermore, the plant's antifungal activity, against species such as Aspergillus and Fusarium, is likely due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds that can inhibit fungal growth and development. The plant's antibacterial properties, against species such as Staphylococcus and Escherichia, are also attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds that can inhibit bacterial growth and proliferation.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Cleisostoma lendyanum are flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, quercetin and kaempferol being the two major flavonoids responsible for these effects. Quercetin has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, while kaempferol has been found to have potent antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Additionally, phenolic acids present in Cleisostoma lendyanum have been found to have antimicrobial and antiviral activities. Alkaloids present in the plant may also have a role in its medicinal properties, although their specific effects have not been fully characterized. The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities of these constituents may contribute to the plant's potential use in treating various health conditions, including inflammation, infection, and cancer.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Cleisostoma lendyanum are infusion, tea, tincture, decoction and salve. Infusion is prepared by steeping the flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, while tea is made by mixing the dried flowers with hot water. Tincture is created by soaking the flowers in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin for several weeks, allowing for the extraction of bioactive compounds. Decoction is prepared by boiling the flowers in water for 10-15 minutes, often used for its medicinal properties. Salve is a topical preparation made by infusing the flowers in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil for its potential wound-healing and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Cleisostoma lendyanum improperly are cause diarrhea, induce vomiting, induce nausea, trigger allergic reactions, and trigger anaphylaxis. These side effects occur due to the plant's toxic compounds, such as cardenolides and bufadienolides, which can be released when the plant is ingested, inhaled, or comes into contact with the skin. When ingested, these compounds can cause gastrointestinal distress, leading to symptoms like diarrhea, vomiting, and nausea. In severe cases, an allergic reaction or anaphylaxis can occur, which can be life-threatening and requires immediate medical attention. The improper use of Cleisostoma lendyanum can also lead to other systemic effects, such as cardiovascular problems and neurological issues, due to the plant's toxic properties.

taxonomy: Cleisostoma lendyanum is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification places it within the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Coelogyneae. The genus Cleisostoma is a monophyletic group, meaning it consists of closely related species. Cleisostoma lendyanum is also classified as a species of orchid endemic to Sri Lanka. Further specific classification within the genus may require more in-depth research.

intro: Cleisostoma lendyanum is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
